preamble: Nasal palatalization, the shift from the alveolar nasal [n] to the palatal nasal [ɲ], is a highly frequent phonetic process triggered by following high front vowels or the palatal glide [j]. This development is a hallmark of the transition from Latin to the Romance languages and is equally prominent in the Slavic linguistic landscape.

examples.note: The Latin cluster /nj/ palatalized to [ɲ] in most Romance varieties.
Palatalization of /n/ before the front jer /ь/ or /j/.
Assimilation of /n/ with a following /j/ glide results in the palatal nasal /ɲ/ (written 'ny').

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
